title: SCP desktop display contracts
description: Machine-readable contract for the desktop monitor's three display modes. Documents scp.desktop-display-contracts/v1. This is a documentation-backed contract, not a runtime wire payload: it captures the shared invariants, per-mode layout/interaction rules, coexistence policy, and the v1 capability boundary for the wallboard. The canonical machine IDs are right-dock (Side Monitor), workbench (Workbench, default primary), and wallboard (Wallboard); implementations MUST use these IDs verbatim in configuration, telemetry, and this schema (docs/desktop-display-contracts.md §4, §8.1). The desktop is a read-only observer of SCP run views (scp.run-view/v1); it never writes run artifacts. Where the underlying data model cannot yet honestly support a feature, the contract says so in deferredCapabilities rather than letting the UI fake it. Cross-artifact vocabulary (mode IDs, enum tokens, required state keys, shared scope, deferred capabilities, hero metrics) uses the canonical literals from docs/desktop-display-contracts.md and the desktop app's display TS types verbatim.

schemaVersion.description: Numeric version of the desktop display contracts document.

schema.description: Versioned contract identifier for the desktop display contracts.

shared.description: Invariants shared by all three display modes (docs §5). Every mode must honor these regardless of observation distance.

dataHonesty.description: Rules keeping the UI truthful (docs §5.1). A Session maps to one scheduling run (ScpRunView); a Subagent maps to a single Claude task (ScpTaskView / ScpActiveTask). No fabricated live token rates, throughput, or 'effective productivity' figures.

sessionMapping.description: How a Session is represented (one ScpRunView).

subagentMapping.description: How a Subagent is represented (one ScpTaskView / ScpActiveTask).

forbiddenFabrications.description: Effects/figures that must never be invented to enhance perceived busyness.

renderViewModelDirectly.description: True when the desktop must render the SCP view model directly and never read raw run artifacts.

tokenPresentation.description: Honest token display rules (docs §5.2). Show precise values only when tokenEvidence.measured === true; otherwise label explicitly as 'not measured' / 'run-level aggregate only'. No fake zero values when data is missing. Mode 3 hero token display uses accumulated measured totals only; no synthetic token stream or rate in v1.

preciseOnlyWhenMeasured.description: Whether precise values are shown only when measured evidence is present.

missingDataLabel.description: Copy used when token data is missing, e.g. 'not measured' / '未测量'.

aggregateOnlyLabel.description: Copy used when only run-level aggregation is available, e.g. 'run-level aggregate only' / '仅有运行级聚合'.

noFakeZero.description: Whether fake zero values are forbidden when data is absent.

noSyntheticTokenRate.description: Whether synthetic real-time token rate/throughput is forbidden in v1.

reducedMotion.description: Reduced motion is a hard requirement across all modes, including the wallboard (docs §5.3). It degrades rather than disabling the UI; Mode 1/2 lose no information under reduced motion.

required.description: Whether reduced motion support is mandatory.

largeScreenAlsoSupported.description: Whether the wallboard/large-screen mode must also honor reduced motion.

degradationBehavior.description: How motion degrades: 'static' = freeze ambient motion and keep data updates; 'minimal' = keep only essential transitions; 'disabled' = drop all motion.

nodeStateViaNonMotion.description: When true, node state is expressed via brightness/border/text only, never motion alone.

accessibility.description: Keyboard reachability and non-color signaling rules (docs §5.4). High-priority state must never rely on color alone; at least one of position/border/icon/text must accompany it. right-dock and workbench must have a complete keyboard path; wallboard MAY use canvas but core information must also exist as a DOM/aria mirror.

keyboardReachableModes.description: Modes that must have a full keyboard path (right-dock and workbench).

nonColorSignalingRequired.description: Whether high-priority state requires a non-color secondary signal.

canvasMustHaveDomMirror.description: Whether canvas regions must keep a DOM/aria text mirror of core information.

progressiveDisclosureDefault.description: Whether glance-first rendering with progressive disclosure into detail is the default.

localization.description: Bilingual (zh/en) and formatting rules (docs §5.5). Locale switching, time formatting, and number formatting must all route through one shared layer; no mixed languages or format drift. Waiting-class states must name the next action.

singleFormatterLayer.description: Whether time/number/locale formatting all go through one shared utility layer.

copyMustBeBilingual.description: Whether every copy key must ship complete in both locales.

noMixedLanguage.description: Whether mixing languages within a single view is forbidden.

waitingStatesMustNameNextAction.description: Whether waiting-class states must name the next action, e.g. 'Waiting on you — approve shell access'.

disconnectedBehavior.description: Behavior when the bridge connection is lost (docs §5.6). Degradation is allowed, but a full blank screen or silent display of stale data is forbidden. The UI must show an explicit disconnected banner stating the source time of the last known state.

blankScreenForbidden.description: Whether showing a fully blank screen is forbidden.

silentStaleDataForbidden.description: Whether silently showing stale data without a marker is forbidden.

explicitDisconnectedBannerRequired.description: Whether an explicit disconnected banner with last-known-state time is required.

regionalDegradePreferred.description: Whether connection errors should degrade regions rather than the whole page.

pollingFallback.description: Whether the desktop falls back to /health + /runs polling after SSE loss.

freshnessThresholds.description: Freshness / staleness thresholds (docs §5.7, §8.5). The run view exposes stalenessMs, lastHeartbeatAt, lastUsefulEventAt; the desktop compares these against a single shared threshold source rather than each mode inventing its own. The threshold source and the freshness indicator are shared single-source utilities across all three modes.

singleThresholdSource.description: Whether staleness thresholds come from one shared source.

staleMs.description: Milliseconds after which a run with no forward progress is considered stale (STALE).

fields.description: Run-view fields used to compute freshness, e.g. ['stalenessMs','lastHeartbeatAt','lastUsefulEventAt'].

modes.description: Per-mode contracts keyed by canonical mode ID (docs §4, §8.1). Exactly these three keys are valid in v1.

coexistence.description: Window coexistence policy (docs §7). The three modes are observation distances, not mutually exclusive windows. right-dock and workbench may coexist; wallboard is an independent observation window that does not steal local selection state from the others. All coexisting windows fan out internally from the single bridge connection.

defaultPrimaryMode.description: The mode that opens as the default main window. Canonical value: workbench.

sharedAcrossWindows.description: Global state shared when right-dock + workbench coexist (docs §7).

notSharedAcrossWindows.description: Local state that stays per-window when right-dock + workbench coexist (docs §7).

wallboardIsIndependent.description: Whether wallboard acts as an independent observation window.

wallboardDoesNotStealSelection.description: Whether wallboard avoids stealing right-dock / workbench local selection.

wallboardReadsGlobalStateOnly.description: Whether wallboard reads only global run state and the event stream.

switchEntryPoints.description: UI entry points where mode switching is exposed (docs §7).

singleBridgeConnection.description: Whether multi-window UIs fan-out internally from one bridge connection rather than opening one each.

deferredCapabilities.description: Capabilities that the current scp.* data model cannot honestly support in v1 (docs §1.1). The UI must not fake these; they require new schema fields from the main plugin. The wallboard v2 boundary depends on these. The unsupportedInV1 token set is bijective with the desktop app's deferred capability IDs.

unsupportedInV1.description: Features that cannot be made real on the current contract and must not be faked.

wallboardV2Needs.description: New schema fields the main plugin should add to enable a full wallboard v2 conductor narrative.

wallboardV2DeferredEffects.description: Visual effects that are visual guesses without new schema and must be deferred to wallboard v2 (docs §8.6 v1 honesty boundary).

modeIdEnum.description: Canonical display mode IDs (docs §4, §8.1). Implementations MUST use these verbatim.

modeRoleEnum.description: Mode role. right-dock = companion, workbench = primary, wallboard = showcase (docs §4 'Default?' column).

autoExpandPolicyEnum.description: right-dock panel auto-expand policy (docs §4.1, §8.3). Pinned default: selectedSessionOnly.

requiredStateKeyEnum.description: Required state keys every mode must define up front (docs §6).

coexistenceSharedScopeEnum.description: State shared across coexisting mode windows (docs §7).

coexistenceLocalScopeEnum.description: State each window keeps locally, not shared (docs §7).

notificationTriggerEnum.description: Triggers permitted to raise a peripheral notification (docs §4.1, §8.4).

heroMetricEnum.description: Hero metrics wallboard v1 may honestly show, limited to three (docs §4.3).

deferredCapabilityEnum.description: Deferred capability identifiers the underlying scp.* schemas cannot yet express (docs §1.1). wallboard v2 is gated on the main plugin adding the corresponding schema fields.

healthRibbonMetricIdEnum.description: Health ribbon metric IDs (docs §4.2, §8.5). DONE_TOTAL represents the DONE / TOTAL count pair. Each must declare its value provenance.

notificationPolicy.description: Per-mode notification policy (docs §8.4). right-dock: taskbar/Dock badge count + system notifications restricted to failed/blocked/waiting-on-user + optional subtle sound (default off). workbench: in-app region-level alerts, no system notifications by default, errors degrade a region not the page. wallboard: alert rail + event ticker for high-priority items, no desktop system notifications.

badgeCount.description: Whether a taskbar/Dock badge count of runs needing attention is shown (right-dock).

systemNotifications.description: Whether OS/system notifications are enabled. right-dock: true (restricted to notifyOn); workbench/wallboard: false.

inAppRegionAlerts.description: Whether in-app region-level alerts are used instead of system notifications (workbench).

alertRailAndTicker.description: Whether an alert rail + event ticker surfaces high-priority items (wallboard).

notifyOn.description: Event classes that may raise a notification.

defaultOff.description: Whether the sound is off by default.

errorsDegradeRegionNotPage.description: Whether connection errors degrade a region rather than the whole page (workbench).

healthRibbonMetric.description: A single always-on health ribbon metric. Each metric is clickable to filter the left list and MUST declare which view-model field(s) it is derived from (docs §8.5 provenance requirement).

derivedFrom.description: View-model field(s) this metric is derived from, e.g. ACTIVE <- run/task status; STALE <- stalenessMs vs stale threshold; BLOCKED <- task.status (blocked/waiting-on-user); FAILED <- task.status (failed); DONE_TOTAL <- task counts (completed vs total).

clickToFilter.description: Whether clicking the metric filters the left run list.

stateCopyKeys.description: Required state copy keys / state identifiers that every mode must define up front rather than improvising at implementation time (docs §6). Includes first-run, all-quiet, disconnected, last-known, and recovered states with their recommended bilingual copy tone.

requiredKeys.description: State identifiers each mode must define.

copy.description: Recommended unified copy tone keyed by state identifier. Values are bilingual sample strings.

noActiveSessions.description: e.g. 'No active sessions / 当前没有活动会话'.

connectionLost.description: e.g. 'Connection lost — showing last known state from HH:MM'.

showingLastKnownState.description: Explicit stale/last-known-state helper copy when the UI is frozen on prior data.

awaitingDirection.description: e.g. 'All agents stopped — awaiting direction / 所有子代理已停止 — 等待新的调度'.

rightDockMode.description: right-dock — Side Monitor: a resident, low-distraction, collapsible right-side monitoring rail (companion window, not a second main panel). Answers 'is Claude working right now?' and 'does any session need a glance?' (docs §4.1).

isDefault.description: right-dock is an optional companion, not the default primary.

geometry.description: Dock geometry and material. Width must be configurable and persisted.

dockSide.description: Docked to the screen right edge, full height.

collapsedRailPx.description: Width of the collapsed status rail (48px).

expandedWidthPx.description: Expanded dock width range (docs §4.1: default 360, min 300, max 400).

material.description: Default transparent frosted glass; black frosted suits low-light.

widthPersisted.description: Whether the configured width is persisted across sessions.

informationArchitecture.description: Three stacked layers: status bar, session stack, subagent drilldown.

sessionStackStatusDotAligned.description: Whether session status dots sit on one vertical line.

autoExpandPolicy.description: Panel auto-expand policy for right-dock (docs §4.1, §8.3). Must have an explicit config item with a pinned default. Default-expanded = most-recent active/running/blocked session; subagents collapsed by default; collapsing a session collapses its subagents; new events refresh dot + timestamp only (no force-expand).

subagentsDefaultCollapsed.description: Whether subagent rows are collapsed by default.

collapseSessionCollapsesSubagents.description: Whether collapsing a session also collapses its subagents.

newEventDoesNotForceExpand.description: Whether new events only refresh the status dot and update time.

notificationPolicy.description: Peripheral alert policy (docs §8.4). right-dock uses badge count + restricted system notifications + optional sound (default off).

workbenchMode.description: workbench — Workbench: the default primary workbench (controller-first, dense but calm, readable under stress). The main window for watching builds, chasing problems, and reading detail (docs §4.2).

isDefault.description: workbench is the default primary main window.

layout.description: Title/Connection bar -> Health Ribbon -> three columns -> Footer (docs §4.2, §8.2).

columns.description: Three-column body: left run list, center detail+table+inspection, right events+metrics.

center.description: Center column width, e.g. '1fr' (Run Detail + Task Table + Task Inspection).

right.description: Right column width, e.g. '260px' (Recent Events + Metrics Rail).

degradeBreakpoints.description: Width-based degradation breakpoints, ordered by descending width (docs §4.2, §8.2).

maxWidthPx.description: Below this width the behavior applies.

healthRibbon.description: Always-on health ribbon (docs §4.2, §8.5). Always present, not gated on a selected run. Each metric is a filter into the left list and MUST declare its value provenance (which view-model field it is derived from) before implementation.

alwaysPresent.description: Whether the health ribbon is always present, not gated on a selected run.

metrics.description: Health ribbon metrics with their value provenance.

centerWorkbench.description: Center column splits into Run Header, Task Table, Selected Task Inspection so the user can see run, task, and task detail without jumping (docs §4.2, §8.3).

interactionModel.description: Stable, predictable interaction (docs §4.2). Selected run/task must stick; Follow live must be explicit and visible; manual scroll-back surfaces Jump to latest.

focusStickiness.description: Whether the selected run/task sticks and is not auto-stolen by new events.

explicitlyVisible.description: Whether Follow live is explicitly visible.

jumpToLatestOnScrollBack.description: Whether manual scroll-back surfaces a Jump to latest control.

regionalDegradeOnError.description: Whether connection errors degrade regions rather than blanking the page.

copyStyle.description: Quiet Cockpit style: verb phrases over bare enums; warning tone is reminder-style, not screaming; failure uses a two-clause 'what happened + what happens next'. Copy must be fully bilingual.

samplePhrases.description: Sample bilingual verb-phrase states, e.g. 'Working / 正在处理'.

notificationPolicy.description: Notification policy (docs §8.4). workbench uses in-app region-level alerts; no system notifications by default; errors degrade a region, not the page.

wallboardMode.description: wallboard — Wallboard: a single-window large-screen showcase wallboard (ambient oversight, demos, second monitor). Tells the story 'Codex is orchestrating many Claude subagents' with real dynamics, not fine-grained operation (docs §4.3).

isDefault.description: wallboard is a presentation board, not the default primary.

capabilityTiers.description: v1/v2 capability boundary (docs §1.1, §8.6). v1 builds a real wallboard on the current contract; v2 waits for main-plugin schema extension to do the full conductor/handoff narrative.

v1.description: What wallboard v1 may honestly build on the current contract.

v2.description: What wallboard v2 will require from main-plugin schema extension. See deferredCapabilities.wallboardV2Needs.

renderingStrategy.description: Mixed rendering, not full-page canvas (docs §4.3). Motion/atmosphere on canvas; hero metrics, session clusters, alerts, events, and token/cost numbers in DOM. Canvas regions MUST provide a DOM/aria text mirror.

stageLayout.description: Stage is a vertical stack of bands (docs §4.3, §8.2). commandBar (~7%) and heroMetrics (~15%) sit at the top; statusRibbon (~4%) at the bottom; the bodyRow between them splits horizontally into sessionClusters (~22%), agentSwarm (~48%), alertsEvents (~30%) which sum to ~100% of the body row. Top/bottom band percentages are of the viewport height; body-row column percentages are of the body-row width — they are NOT on the same axis and must not be summed together.

commandBarPct.description: Top command bar band, % of viewport height (~7%).

heroMetricsBandPct.description: Top hero metrics band, % of viewport height (~15%).

bodyRow.description: Horizontal split of the body row. The three columns sum to ~100% of the body-row width, NOT the viewport.

sessionClustersPct.description: Left session clusters, % of body-row width (~22%).

agentSwarmPct.description: Center agent swarm / conductor core, % of body-row width (~48%).

alertsEventsPct.description: Right alerts + events, % of body-row width (~30%).

statusRibbonPct.description: Bottom status ribbon / event ticker band, % of viewport height (~4%).

heroMetrics.description: Hero metrics, kept to three honest, contract-derivable values (docs §4.3).

motionSystem.description: Two motion layers. The three primary modes users must recognize, and ambient motion that stays subordinate to them.

reducedMotionDegradation.description: Reduced motion for the wallboard degrades to (docs §5.3, §8.6): static conductor core, no particles/drift/orbit, hero metrics still update, event ticker becomes a static list, node state via brightness/border/text only.

canvasDomMirror.description: Required DOM/aria text mirror for canvas regions, so core info is never canvas-only (docs §5.4). The mirror MUST include at minimum: current active agent count, current list of abnormal agents, the most recent 5 high-priority events, and the current wallboard status.

failureTheater.description: Failure theater is allowed but must not be cheap (docs §8.6). Problem nodes shift cold-to-warm-amber plus non-color signals; the conductor core dims and pulls a thin thread to the problem node. No red-screen alarms, no full-screen flashing, no color-only changes. Bare 'Awaiting direction.' without context is forbidden.

awaitingDirectionNeedsContext.description: Whether 'Awaiting direction.' copy must always carry explicit context, e.g. 'All agents stopped — awaiting direction'.

smallWindowDegrade.description: Wallboard is inherently unsuited to small windows; degradation breakpoints ordered by descending width (docs §8.6).

maxWidthPx.description: Below this width the behavior applies (~1280px, ~980px).

performanceBudget.description: Performance budget constraints to write into implementation early (docs §8.6).

targetFps.description: Target frames per second under long runs (60).

agentClusteringThreshold.description: Whether an agent-count clustering threshold must be defined.

canvasNodeOrParticleCap.description: Whether a canvas node/particle cap must be defined.

longRunMemoryLeakMonitoring.description: Whether a long-run memory-leak monitoring plan must exist.

notificationPolicy.description: Notification policy (docs §8.4). wallboard uses an alert rail + event ticker for high-priority items; no desktop system notifications.
